location_moodboards/virginia_slave_cabin.prompt: Cinematic empty-scene location reference photograph, no people. Inspired by surviving antebellum slave quarters in Tidewater Virginia — a single-room dwelling of hand-hewn pine logs chinked with red clay, a packed dirt floor, and a tin roof patched with flattened cans. A lone kerosene lantern is the only light source, throwing a tight amber pool over a straw-tick mattress while the corners drown in tar-black shadow; rain on tin provides a percussive, almost heartbeat ambience. Surfaces are tactile and rough: splintered rafters hung with drying herbs, a soot-blackened hearth, coarse linen sheets gone gray from lye washing, a tin basin beaded with sweat-water. The palette is candle-amber, ink-black, ember-red, wet-pine brown. Camera-friendly notes: tight, low-ceilinged interior favors close lenses (28–40mm) and chiaroscuro one-light setups; practicals can motivate every beat, and the dark beyond the doorway reads as a pure black void for mythic silhouettes. Lightning flashes outside can punch quick blue counter-key without breaking the lantern world.. Palette: lantern amber, tar black, ember red, wet-pine brown. Anamorphic, photorealistic, no text/logos.

location_moodboards/fields_mills_road_mo.prompt: Cinematic empty-scene location reference photograph, no people. A composite Reconstruction-era Southern landscape drawn from Piedmont cotton country, Carolina pine sawmills, and the rutted clay roads that fed the C&O rail expansion. Light is wide and unforgiving: a high, bleaching noon sun over the cotton rows, then dust-filtered God-rays slanting through a sawmill's open-sided shed, finally a hazy late-afternoon orange where coal smoke from distant camps smudges the horizon. Materials shift across the montage — cotton bolls like dirty popcorn, hand-split cypress shingles, rough oak axe handles polished by palm sweat, a rusted cider barrel sweating sap, the iron-and-coal grit of the road. The palette runs sun-bleached white, cotton-cream, sawdust-gold, iron-rust, distant-smoke blue. Camera-friendly notes: built for sweeping wides and long-lens heat-shimmer compressions; a slow dolly past sawmill blades, backlit dust, and a hero crossroads tableau with John Henry centered against converging horizons make natural montage punctuation. Wind in the cotton and floating sawdust give every frame organic motion.. Palette: sun-bleached white, sawdust gold, iron rust, smoke blue. Anamorphic, photorealistic, no text/logos.

location_moodboards/brush_arbor_and_red_.prompt: Cinematic empty-scene location reference photograph, no people. Modeled on the brush-arbor and pine-board praise houses of the post-Emancipation Carolinas — an open-sided shelter of rough pine slats and a roof of fresh-cut pine boughs, set in a longleaf clearing off a clay road the color of dried blood. Light is the hero: hard Sunday-noon sun slices between board gaps in clean parallel blades, raking the swaying congregation with stripes of gold and shadow and lighting suspended motes of pollen and dust. Surfaces smell as much as they look — turpentine weeping from green pine, sweat-darkened hat brims, hymnal paper soft as cloth, hand-stitched yellow kerchief, the iron hinge of a weather-silvered gate. Outside, the red dirt road glows terracotta between walls of loblolly pine whose long late shadows stripe the lovers as they walk. Palette: pine-sap gold, kerchief yellow, clay red, shadow indigo, Sunday white. Camera-friendly notes: the slatted walls are a natural cucoloris for in-camera god-rays; shoot the duet in matched singles with shallow depth to braid the voices visually, then transition to a wide two-shot on the road using the pine-shadow bars as a built-in rhythm track. Golden hour on the clay road needs almost no augmentation.. Palette: pine-sap gold, clay red, kerchief yellow, shadow indigo, Sunday white. Anamorphic, photorealistic, no text/logos.

lookbook/style_frame.png.prompt: A 65mm anamorphic-aspect film still, 2.39:1, of a newborn Black infant cradled in the arms of a young mother seated on a low rope-strung bed inside a single-room hand-hewn pine-log slave cabin in Tidewater Virginia, 1865. The walls are chinked with red Virginia clay, the floor is packed earth, a patched tin roof overhead. A single tallow candle on a rough plank table is the only light source — its flame motivates a warm amber key on the mother's cheekbone and the baby's brow, falling off rapidly into deep, unlifted shadow that swallows the back of the cabin. Skin tones rendered rich and luminous, like a Gordon Parks portrait crossed with a Caravaggio chiaroscuro. Heavy atmosphere — woodsmoke and dust catching the candlelight in slow vertical drift. Composition is centered, symmetrical, with generous headroom and the candle flame placed on a lower third; shallow depth of field on a 50mm spherical prime at T2.0, focus on the infant's closed eyelids. Color palette: oxblood red clay, tobacco brown timber, bone-white linen swaddle, candle-amber highlight, ink-black negative space. Crushed shadows, creamy highlight rolloff, fine 65mm grain, a whisper of halation around the flame. Mythic, hushed, ominous — the opening frame of an American folk gospel.

narration/s02_growing_up.mp3.prompt: He grew like an unshaded hickory — straight, hard, reaching for sky no one had promised him. Something inside me said stop, boy. Rest. But a dollar and a quarter a day don't whisper — it hollers.

frames/s03_brush_arbor/s03_05.compositio.prompt:
VISUAL STYLE: anamorphic, practical light, teal-and-orange grade, 35mm grain, shallow depth of field.
LENS: Spherical primes on large-format digital (ARRI Alexa 65 or LF) to mimic the grain-and-grandeur of 65mm stock: Panavision Sphero 65 / DNA LF set — 24mm, 29mm, 35mm, 50mm, 75mm, 100mm. Lean heavily on the 35mm and 50mm for waist-up portraiture; reserve the 24mm for landscape vistas and the 100mm for compressed heat-haze montages. Minimal zooms. Glass chosen for gentle field curvature and warm uncoated flares from oil-lamp and lantern practicals..
GRADE: A warm, earth-pigment palette — oxblood reds, tobacco browns, longleaf-pine greens, and bone-white skies — printed with crushed shadows, creamy highlight rolloff, and a subtle silver-retention bite in the midtones so skin reads like worked leather and iron..

PHOTOREALISTIC CINEMATIC FILM STILL.

SHOT: LS (low, three-quarter rear angle), ~40mm lens.
CAMERA: starts slow dolly behind them.

LOCATION: Brush-arbor church and red dirt road, the Carolinas. Modeled on the brush-arbor and pine-board praise houses of the post-Emancipation Carolinas — an open-sided shelter of rough pine slats and a roof of fresh-cut pine boughs, set in a longleaf clearing off a clay road the color of dried blood. Light is the hero: hard Sunday-noon sun slices between boar
TIME: Hot Sunday

CHARACTERS:
  - John Henry — monumental Black laborer in his early thirties, granite-jawed, quietly thunderous. Towering, broad-chested Black steel driver born on a Virginia plantation in the 1840s; calm-eyed, quiet, with skillet-sized hands. After Emancipation he follows the railroad camps, becomes the legendary hammer-swinger at Big Bend Tunnel.
  - Polly Ann — petite, fiery Black woman in her late twenties with a singer's open chest and flashing eyes. John Henry's small, fierce wife with a yellow kerchief, a laugh that fills clearings and a temper that empties them. Mother, singer, and steel driver in her own right.

ACTION: They walk side by side down a red dirt road. Pine shadows long across the clay. Neither speaks. Her hand brushes his once.
COMPOSITION: Golden hour. His silhouette dwarfs hers. Long pine shadows striping the road. Cicadas, no dialogue. The brush of hands is the whole shot.
MOOD: Warm, tender, fated

NEGATIVE: no text, no logos, no UI, no watermarks, no illustration.
